items.title: President Trump: US could ‘take the oil in Iran’
items.summary: Reuters is reporting the latest comments delivered by US President Donald Trump in his interview with the Financial Times (FT).

items.title: BoJ Summary of Opinions: Members split on rate hike prospects amid Middle East war
items.summary: The Bank of Japan (BoJ) published its Summary of Opinions of the March monetary policy meeting, with the key takeaways found below.

items.title: WTI retests $100 as the winning streak extends alongside Middle East war
items.summary: West Texas Intermediate (WTI) – the US oil benchmark – witnesses a fresh leg higher in early dealings on Monday, extending its winning streak into a fourth consecutive trading day.

items.title: ECB’s Villeroy: Policymakers are ready to act if energy-driven inflation broadens
items.summary: European Central Bank (ECB) Governing Council (GC) member and Bank of France Governor, François Villeroy de Galhau made some comments on the monetary policy in his speech early Monday.

items.title: Israel Defense Forces intercepted two unmanned aerial vehicles launched from Yemen
items.summary: In its latest post on X, the Israel Defense Forces (IDF). the nation’s military, said that “in the past hour, the Air Force intercepted two unmanned aerial vehicles launched from Yemen.”

items.title: Japanese Yen keeps falling as the Middle East war enters fifth week
items.summary: The Japanese Yen (JPY) continues to remain at the receiving when compared to the US Dollar (USD) as a new week begins in Asia on Monday.

items.title: Australian Dollar opens lower as Iran warns against US ground military action
items.summary: The Australian Dollar (AUD) trades lower against its major currency peers at the start of the week, trading 0.27% lower to near 0.6850 against the US Dollar (USD) in the opening trade.

items.title: Thai Baht: War-driven pressures challenge BOT stance – DBS
items.summary: DBS Group Research economist Chua Han Teng highlights that Thailand’s financial markets, particularly the Thai Baht (THB) and equities, are under pressure due to vulnerability to Middle East conflict-related commodity shocks.

items.title: Philippines: Extended pause view after BSP off-cycle move – UOB
items.summary: UOB’s Global Economics & Markets Research, via Julia Goh and Loke Siew Ting, notes that the central bank of the Philippines, Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas (BSP) kept the RRP (Reverse Repurchase Rate) rate at 4.25% in an off-cycle meeting as supply-driven inflation and Middle East risks intensify.

items.title: CNY: PBoC seen resisting strong appreciation – Commerzbank
items.summary: Commerzbank economists Dr. Henry Hao and Volkmar Baur say China’s industrial profits surged early in 2026, led by AI-related electronics, but this strength predates the recent energy shock.

items.title: Indonesia: Inflation pressures from Oil and festivals – DBS
items.summary: DBS Group Research expects Indonesia’s March CPI inflation to stay firm at 4% year-on-year, slightly below February’s 4.8%, but with a faster monthly pace. Analysts highlight the impact of higher energy prices and festive demand, as well as base effects.

items.title: India: Energy shock risks and policy playbook – Societe Generale
items.summary: Societe Generale’s Kunal Kundu analyses how the Iran conflict exposes India’s macro vulnerabilities through imported energy dependence and trade route risks. Kundu highlights broad spillovers from higher Oil and gas prices into the consumption basket and external balances.
